sections.paragraph:
ἔγκειμαι (Hom. et al.; ins, pap, LXX, Test12Patr, Joseph.) to be emphatic about someth., insist, warn urgently (so Hdt. 7, 158, 1; Thu. 2, 59, 2; Herodas 5, 3; Plut., Fab. Max. 179 [9, 2]; Lucian, Demon. 12; Jos., Vi. 19, Ant. 15, 31) MPol 9:3.—ITr 12:3 cj. (for περίκειμαι).—DELG s.v. κεῖμαι.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

Herodas Herodas , a.k.a. Herondas, III b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

  1. cj. = conjecture(d by)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 273
